methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $900M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Nuclear Energy (found 1 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 4 same-sector peers exist in Nuclear Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Pacific Fusion, a Series A nuclear energy company founded by Eric Lander and Will Regan, is being valued within a sector comprising 3 public comparables (OKLO, NNE, SMR) and 8 private peers including Fuse Energy, X-Energy, and Nano Nuclear Energy. The company has raised $900M to date.

The stage-progression method is the applicable triangulation approach. Using $900M in total raised as a valuation proxy and applying a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A progression yields an implied valuation of $1.8B. This method directly reflects observed mark-up patterns as frontier nuclear ventures move through financing stages. Peer-median valuation could not be applied due to insufficient narrow-stage comparables within nuclear energy; while 4 same-sector peers exist, none met the threshold of 3 datapoints when filtered for same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar valuation tier.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $1.5B (low) to $2.1B (high), centered on $1.8B. The range reflects near-term sensitivity to capital deployment pace and technical milestone achievement. Key uncertainties include the stage-progression multiplier's appropriateness in a frontier sector where risk and time-to-value may diverge from venture norms, and the limited comparable set available for cross-validation. Expansion of the peer-median approach would require closer monitoring of Series A and Series B closings within nuclear energy.
